text.title: AI-Generated Code Lint Presets Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Did an AI PR modify `ruff-ai-presets.toml` or `eslint-ai-presets.json`?

conclusion.verdict: reject the PR; the AI is forbidden from editing its own lint

branch.question: Does a test contain only `assert True` or `expect(result)`?
Does a `try/except: pass` or empty `catch` appear?

conclusion.verdict: fail lint; require narrow exception + log + re-raise

branch.question: Is a timeout/retry/threshold literal hard-coded in production code?

conclusion.verdict: replace with a named constant; lint blocks the commit
fail lint; route the diagnostic through the project logger
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
